title: Alpha playtest kit and observation protocol
summary: Prepare consistent external alpha playtest sessions without changing gameplay: run/install handoff, save reset and console checklist, science comprehension prompts, spatial observation checklist, and route/support observation checklist.

execution_order.goal: Prepare lane 1's run/install/save-reset and RC artifact handoff for a non-developer alpha session.
execution_order.why_now: Players and observers need a repeatable way to start the alpha without debugging the repo.

execution_order.goal: Prepare lane 2's kid-comprehension and science-understanding prompts.
execution_order.why_now: The playtest should learn what players understand, not only whether the app runs.
The alpha's strongest question is whether children remember places and traversal beats.
The route loop needs external proof that active, ready-to-file, filed, and support-choice states read clearly.

execution_order.goal: Implement lane 1's playtest run sheet, save-reset instructions, RC artifact location, and console/crash checklist.
execution_order.why_now: This gives an observer a practical session-start protocol.

execution_order.goal: Implement lane 2's comprehension prompts and adult observer science notes.
execution_order.why_now: Learning evidence should be captured consistently across sessions.

execution_order.goal: Implement lane 3's place-memory and visual/spatial hesitation checklist.
execution_order.why_now: The observer should know where to watch for readable versus confusing places.

execution_order.goal: Implement lane 4's route/support state observation checklist.
execution_order.why_now: The observer should capture motivation and confusion around route filing, replay, and support choices.

execution_order.goal: Review the lane 1 playtest run sheet for practicality and repeatability.
execution_order.why_now: The kit should be usable by someone who did not build the game.

execution_order.goal: Review the lane 2 comprehension prompts for age fit and science accuracy.
execution_order.why_now: Questions should invite evidence without leading children to the answer.

execution_order.goal: Review the lane 3 spatial checklist for observable place-memory signals.
execution_order.why_now: The spatial rubric should be concrete enough for repeated sessions.

execution_order.goal: Review the lane 4 route/support checklist for active/ready/filed clarity.
execution_order.why_now: The playtest should expose route-loop confusion before beta expansion.

avoid: new screenshots unless the checklist truly needs an example

owns: active versus ready-to-file versus filed clarity prompts

completion_gate: A non-developer observer can start an alpha session from the RC artifact or local build instructions.
The kit explains how to reset or prepare save state without editing source.
Observer notes cover install/run, comprehension, science learning, spatial hesitation, place memory, route/support clarity, and motivation.
No gameplay, content, geometry, route, station, save, or support behavior changes are introduced by packet 159.
guardrails: Do not begin Source to Shore implementation in packet 159.
Do not change gameplay or content to satisfy the playtest kit.
Keep the kit practical for 6-10 observed sessions.
Prefer checklists, rubrics, and session notes over new UI.
Packet 160 should triage actual playtest results before beta campaign work opens.
post_packet_path: Packet 160 should classify real session feedback and choose harden alpha, onboarding pass, Source to Shore beta, or another direction.
Source to Shore packets 161-176 remain seeded until packet 160 chooses them.
